proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 (binary) is positively associated with objective_response: patients with feature_014=1 have a higher response rate than feature_014=0.
feature_001 (binary) is positively associated with objective_response: feature_001=1 patients have a higher response rate.
feature_021 (binary) is positively associated with objective_response: feature_021=1 patients have a higher response rate.
feature_004 (3-level categorical) is associated with objective_response: response rate decreases monotonically as feature_004 goes 0 -> 1 -> 2.
feature_020 (continuous) is negatively associated with objective_response: higher feature_020 values predict lower response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 23.5% in feature_014=1 vs 16.7% in feature_014=0; chi-square p<1e-69, log-OR=+0.42.
Response rate 22.1% in feature_001=1 vs 16.5% in feature_001=0; chi-square p<1e-54, log-OR=+0.36.
Response rate 22.0% in feature_021=1 vs 16.6% in feature_021=0; chi-square p<1e-50, log-OR=+0.35.
Response rate 23.1% (level 0, n=17592) vs 17.4% (level 1, n=24971) vs 12.9% (level 2, n=7437); monotonic decrease; chi-square p~1.8e-90.
Standardized logistic coefficient = -0.138 (each SD increase reduces log-odds of response by 0.14); p<1e-30.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_024 (continuous) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_029 (continuous, narrow range 1.7-5.5) is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_026 (binary) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_009 (binary) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_027 (continuous) is negatively associated with objective_response.
feature_022 (continuous) is negatively associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Standardized logistic coefficient = -0.094 per SD; p~2.4e-16.
Standardized logistic coefficient = +0.059 per SD; p~2.9e-7.
Response 16.8% in feature_026=1 vs 19.1% in feature_026=0; log-OR=-0.16, p~3.3e-6.
Response 16.6% in feature_009=1 vs 19.0% in feature_009=0; log-OR=-0.17, p~3.5e-5.
Standardized logistic coefficient = -0.059 per SD; p~6e-6.
Standardized logistic coefficient = -0.046 per SD; p~1.9e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 and feature_008 (both binary, both with prevalence 7545/50000) have an unusually correlated joint distribution suggesting redundancy or shared definition.
feature_011 (continuous, range 18-92, plausible age) shows no significant association with objective_response.

Most binary features (feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_006, feature_032, feature_018, feature_008, feature_030) have no marginal association with objective_response (p>0.05).

analyses.result_summary: Cross-tab: (0,0)=36103, (0,1)=6352, (1,0)=6352, (1,1)=1193. Equal off-diagonals, both have marginal n=7545. Strongly correlated but not identical.
Standardized logistic coefficient = -0.0017 per SD; p=0.88. No marginal association.

analyses.code: univariate logistic / chi-square for each binary
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: feature_010 p=0.42, feature_005 p=0.85, feature_016 p=0.64, feature_025 p=0.34, feature_019 p=0.60, feature_015 p=0.47, feature_006 p=0.93, feature_032 p=0.79, feature_018 p=0.67, feature_008 p=0.14, feature_030 p=0.46. None significant marginally.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable logistic regression including all 35 features, the strongest independent predictors of objective_response are feature_004 (3 levels), feature_014, feature_001, feature_021, feature_020, feature_024, feature_029, feature_026, feature_009, feature_027, and feature_022, with the same signs observed univariately.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(np.column_stack([z(cont) for cont in cont_cols] + [df[b] for b in binary_cols] + [feature_004 dummies])))
analyses.result_summary: Fully adjusted model yields: feature_004_lvl2 coef=-0.73 (p=5.5e-76), feature_014 +0.44 (p=1.7e-71), feature_001 +0.37 (p=2.4e-57), feature_021 +0.36 (p=8e-54), feature_004_lvl1 -0.36 (p=1.9e-47), feature_020_z -0.14 (p=2.7e-31), feature_024_z -0.10 (p=1.1e-17), feature_029_z +0.06 (p=1.4e-7), feature_026 -0.17 (p=6e-7), feature_009 -0.19 (p=3.6e-6), feature_027_z -0.06 (p=5.8e-6), feature_022_z -0.05 (p=1.3e-4). Signs unchanged from univariate.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong main-effect binary predictors feature_001, feature_014, and feature_021 are mutually independent in their distribution (phi correlations near 0), so their joint additive effect should approximately sum if they act independently.

analyses.result_summary: Pairwise phi coefficients: feature_001 vs feature_014 phi=0.003 (p=0.48); feature_001 vs feature_021 phi=0.001 (p=0.75); feature_014 vs feature_021 phi=0.001 (p=0.80). All independent in distribution.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_001 and feature_014 on objective_response: response rate when both are 1 exceeds the additive prediction from main effects.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_014 and feature_021 on objective_response.
There is a positive multiplicative interaction between feature_001 and feature_021 on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Two-way interaction logit coefficient feature_001*feature_014 = +1.06, p=6.8e-103. Strong positive synergy.
Two-way interaction logit coefficient feature_014*feature_021 = +1.05, p=5.5e-102. Strong positive synergy.
Two-way interaction logit coefficient feature_001*feature_021 = +0.88, p=1.1e-78. Strong positive synergy.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The pairwise interactions among feature_001, feature_014, feature_021 are driven entirely by a 3-way interaction; in a model that includes the 3-way term, the pairwise terms collapse toward zero. The response gain is concentrated in the triple-positive cell where feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1.

analyses.result_summary: Full saturated model: 3-way interaction coef = +2.13, p=3.9e-92. ALL pairwise interactions collapse to non-significance (f001*f014 p=0.46; f001*f021 p=0.085; f014*f021 p=0.50). Main effects also non-significant individually. The entire response excess is attributable to the triple-positive cell.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 have a markedly higher objective_response rate (~63%) than all other combinations of these three features (which cluster around ~16-17%).

analyses.result_summary: Triple-positive cell (1,1,1): n=2401, response=63.3%. Other 7 cells: response 15.6%-17.2% (range 1.6 percentage points). Triple positive vs all others: chi-square ~ 4500, p effectively 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive (feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1) subgroup, feature_026=1 sharply suppresses the response: response in feature_026=1 patients within the triple-positive subgroup is no higher than baseline (~18%), versus ~72% when feature_026=0.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_026=0 n=2025 response=71.7%; feature_026=1 n=376 response=18.4%. Within-subgroup logistic coef for feature_026 = -2.42, p=4.5e-65. Effectively wipes out the responder signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple-positive subgroup, feature_009=1 also sharply suppresses objective_response: feature_009=1 patients drop to ~19% vs ~69% when feature_009=0.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple-positive: feature_009=0 n=2125 response=69.1%; feature_009=1 n=276 response=18.8%. Within-subgroup logistic coef for feature_009 = -2.26, p=5.8e-45.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 act as independent suppressors of the triple-positive responder signal: each, when set to 1, abolishes most of the response gain associated with the triple-positive kernel; in a model including kernel*feature_026 and kernel*feature_009 the kernel coefficient (+2.86) is offset by approximately equal-magnitude negative interaction coefficients (-2.66 and -2.55).

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(np.column_stack([f001, f014, f021, f026, f009, f001*f014, f001*f021, f014*f021, kernel, kernel*f026, kernel*f009])))
analyses.result_summary: Kernel main coef = +2.86 (p=3.7e-145); kernel*feature_026 = -2.66 (p=2.7e-70); kernel*feature_009 = -2.55 (p=4.4e-50). Net log-odds for triple-positive AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 = +2.86; for triple-positive AND feature_026=1 = +2.86-2.66=+0.20 (~similar to baseline); and for triple-positive AND feature_009=1 = +2.86-2.55=+0.31 (also near baseline). Suppressors are quantitatively cancelling.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The fully specified responder subgroup is feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; objective_response rate in this subgroup is ~78% versus ~16.5% outside.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup n=1790, response 78.6% (1407/1790); complement n=48210, response 16.5% (7968/48210). Fisher OR=18.55, p effectively 0. Difference in proportions = 0.62.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the fully-specified responder subgroup (feature_001=feature_014=feature_021=1, feature_026=feature_009=0), feature_004 still modulates response: level 0 has the highest response and level 2 the lowest, mirroring its baseline gradient.

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup: feature_004=0 n=617 response=86.0%; level 1 n=902 response=77.2%; level 2 n=271 response=66.8%. Within-subgroup logistic coef for feature_004 = -0.56, p=9.6e-11.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup, feature_020 (continuous) continues to negatively modulate response: higher feature_020 values predict lower response, even after the dominant categorical structure is conditioned out.

analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup, standardized feature_020 logistic coef = -0.20, p=4.3e-4. By tertile: low 81.3%, mid 80.0%, high 74.4%.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 'no-marginal-effect' binary features (feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_030) do not reveal a hidden treatment-vs-control effect within the responder subgroup either: response is statistically the same regardless of these variables in the n=1790 subgroup.

analyses.code: for each: fisher_exact within responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within responder subgroup: feature_010 p=0.91, feature_030 p=0.45, feature_005 p=0.74, feature_016 p=0.15, feature_025 p=0.62, feature_019 p=0.11, feature_015 p=0.12, feature_018 p=0.41, feature_008 p=0.69, feature_006 p=1.00, feature_032 p=0.11. None significant at 0.05 after multiple looks. No additional binary treatment-like variable detected.

proposed_hypotheses.text: If we treat feature_014 as the 'treatment' and (feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1) as a putative biomarker pair, the treatment effect of feature_014 is essentially zero outside the biomarker-positive group and very large inside it.

analyses.code: stratified rates: response by feature_014 within (feature_001=feature_021=1) vs others
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1: feature_014=0 response=16.9% (n=5612); feature_014=1 response=63.3% (n=2401). Within this subgroup feature_014 coef ~ +2.2. In all other subgroups feature_014 effect on response is essentially flat (~16-17% regardless). Confirms massive heterogeneity by biomarker pair.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Symmetrically, treating feature_021 as the 'treatment', its effect is concentrated in feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 patients, mirroring h29.
Treating feature_001 as the 'treatment', its effect is concentrated in feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Among feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1: feature_021=0 response=16.0% (n=3585); feature_021=1 response=63.3% (n=2401). 47-percentage-point gap.

Among feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1: feature_001=0 response=15.7% (n=3624); feature_001=1 response=63.3% (n=2401). 48-percentage-point gap. The three features are interchangeable in role for this subgroup definition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Sensitivity check: split the sample randomly in half and re-fit; the responder subgroup definition (feature_001=feature_014=feature_021=1 AND feature_026=feature_009=0) should yield similar response gradients in both halves (~75-80% vs ~16-17%).

analyses.code: np.random.seed(42); split into two halves and compute response rates by mask
analyses.result_summary: Half 1 (n=25000): subgroup n=898, response_in=77.1%, response_out=16.7%, diff=60.4 pp. Half 2 (n=25000): subgroup n=892, response_in=80.2%, response_out=16.4%, diff=63.8 pp. Highly stable across resamples.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment-effect heterogeneity scan: among all features tested as potential modifiers of the triple-positive kernel, feature_026 and feature_009 are by far the strongest negative interaction terms; feature_024 also shows a smaller positive interaction with the kernel (suggesting that within triple-positive patients, higher feature_024 is somewhat preserved).

analyses.code: for each feature: sm.Logit(y, [kernel, feature, kernel*feature])
analyses.result_summary: Top kernel-modifier interactions: feature_026 coef=-2.44 (p=1.8e-62); feature_009 coef=-2.26 (p=4.3e-42); feature_024_z coef=+0.15 (p=5.6e-4); other features p > 0.1. The two binary suppressors dominate; feature_024 has a small additional positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The interaction kernel*feature_004 is borderline (~p=0.05): feature_004 mainly drives a constant additive shift in response rather than truly modifying the kernel effect. By contrast, feature_026 and feature_009 multiplicatively shut down the kernel effect.

analyses.result_summary: Kernel coef = +3.13 (p=4.8e-207); feature_004 main = -0.38 (p=2.3e-92); kernel*feature_004 = -0.17 (p=0.050). Modest interaction at the borderline of significance.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the responder subgroup, no other strong response predictor remains: in the n=48210 complement, modest residual associations exist for feature_004, feature_020, feature_024 but the response rate is approximately flat at 15-19% across feature combinations.

analyses.result_summary: Complement response rates: range 0.152-0.196 across all 31 non-responder strata; no stratum reaches >20%. Confirms the responder subgroup contains essentially all the response-rate heterogeneity attributable to these five binary features.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 and feature_008 (both with marginal n=7545 ones) are not part of the responder kernel and do not modify it (kernel*feature_018, kernel*feature_008 both non-significant in the kernel-modifier scan).

analyses.result_summary: kernel*feature_018: coef +0.17, p=0.19. kernel*feature_008: coef +0.06, p=0.66. Neither modifies the responder kernel.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_004 levels, the responder kernel produces a similar relative log-odds boost; the absolute response in the kernel-positive subgroup is highest in feature_004=0 (~86%), intermediate in feature_004=1 (~77%), and lowest in feature_004=2 (~67%). Outside the kernel, feature_004 produces the established baseline gradient (23/17/13%).

analyses.result_summary: Inside responder subgroup: f004=0 86.0%, f004=1 77.2%, f004=2 66.8%. Outside subgroup: f004=0 20.4%, f004=1 15.0%, f004=2 11.0%. Both inside and outside, lower feature_004 favors response; the relative effect of being in the responder subgroup is roughly +60 percentage points across all feature_004 levels.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis (treating feature_014 as the candidate treatment indicator): the effect of feature_014 on objective_response is concentrated in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; outside that subgroup the effect of feature_014 is approximately zero.
Equivalently (by symmetry), treating feature_001 as the candidate treatment: the effect of feature_001 is concentrated in feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0.
Equivalently, treating feature_021 as the candidate treatment: the effect of feature_021 is concentrated in feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0.

analyses.code: stratified comparisons: feature_X=1 vs feature_X=0 within (other two binaries=1, feature_026=0, feature_009=0) and outside that mask
analyses.result_summary: Treating feature_014 as treatment: within (feature_001=1, feature_021=1, feature_026=0, feature_009=0) feature_014=1 response 78.6% (n=1790) vs feature_014=0 response 16.6% (n=4288); within-subgroup risk difference = +62.0 pp. Outside that subgroup, feature_014's effect on response is essentially flat (16-17% vs 16-17%; within-stratum differences <2 pp). Same logic applies to feature_001 and feature_021 by symmetry. The triad is interchangeable; whichever is regarded as the 'treatment', its effect is concentrated in the other two biomarkers and absent if either suppressor is positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported single subgroup statement: objective_response is dramatically elevated (~78-79%) when feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, vs ~16-17% otherwise. Within this subgroup, lower feature_004 and lower feature_020 further boost response. The remaining 30 features do not contribute additional treatment-effect heterogeneity once these five binary predicates are conditioned upon.

analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup: n=1790, response 78.6% (1407 of 1790) vs complement n=48210, response 16.5% (7968 of 48210). Fisher OR=18.55, p~0. Within-subgroup, feature_004 logistic coef=-0.56, p=9.6e-11; feature_020 standardized coef=-0.20, p=4.3e-4. Outside the subgroup, no feature combination yields response >20%. Complete subgroup specification is feature_001=1 AND feature_014=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0 (with feature_004=0 and lower feature_020 as additional within-subgroup intensifiers).
